evals.prompt: Add JSDoc to the `processUserLogin` function in `auth.ts`
evals.expected_output: The `processUserLogin` function should have a JSDoc block documenting its inputs and outputs without type tags, using the imperative verb form.
assertions: JSDoc block is added to processUserLogin
JSDoc contains @param for username, passwordHash, rememberMe, ipAddress
JSDoc contains @returns tag
JSDoc does not contain any type annotations like {string} or {boolean}
JSDoc description starts with an imperative verb

evals.prompt: Update the jsdoc for all functions in `utils/math.ts`
evals.expected_output: All non-trivial functions in utils/math.ts should have updated JSDoc blocks following the specified rules. Simple getters should be ignored.
assertions: JSDoc block is added to add and calculateCompoundInterest
JSDoc either omitted from getPi, or follows all rules
JSDoc does not contain any type annotations
JSDoc description starts with an imperative verb

evals.prompt: Go through the files changed in the current PR and add jsdoc to any new functions.
evals.expected_output: Only functions that are newly added in the current PR should have JSDoc added/updated, following the specified rules.
assertions: JSDoc block is added to upgradePlan and cancelSubscription
createSubscription JSDoc is left unchanged
JSDoc does not contain any type annotations on newly added docs
JSDoc description starts with an imperative verb on newly added docs

evals.prompt: Go through the files changed in the current PR and ensure jsdoc is updated for both new and existing functions.
evals.expected_output: All functions in the changed files should be checked. Missing JSDoc should be added. Existing JSDoc should only be updated if there is a typo or meaning change, avoiding redundant rephrasings.
assertions: JSDoc block is added to upgradePlan and cancelSubscription
createSubscription JSDoc typo 'typo' is corrected or intent is updated
createSubscription JSDoc is not completely rewritten in a redundant way
JSDoc does not contain any type annotations on any functions

evals.prompt: Add JSDoc to the `parseData` function in `parser.ts`
evals.expected_output: The JSDoc block should include a @throws tag since the function throws an error.
assertions: JSDoc contains @throws tag
JSDoc contains @param and @returns tags
JSDoc does not contain any type annotations
JSDoc description starts with an imperative verb
